claim_bullets.bullet_text: Binding a function with `const` does not name the function; the function remains anonymous (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-78).
A `function` keyword followed by a name creates a named function expression; the name after `function` may differ from the binding name in the environment (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-78).
Functions declared with the `function` keyword must have a block body and use `return` for values (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-78).
The internal name of a function is not necessarily the same as the binding name; this can be confusing (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-78).
JavaScript has a syntax for naming a function with the `function` keyword, which existed before ES2015 (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-78).

source_record_text: Summary of JavaScript function naming conventions from pages 62‑78.
